explanation.rule: Adding -ly to an adjective forms an adverb that describes how, when, or to what extent something happens.

groups.note: The most common adverb suffix in English, added directly to most adjectives

examples.definition: in a way that produces the intended result
in a calm way, without rushing or complaining
in a way that is important or noticeable
slowly, in small stages over time

wordForms.definition: in a way that is sufficient or acceptable
wordForms.example: The staff were not adequately trained for the new system.
I completely forgot about the appointment.

wordForms.definition: in a way that produces the intended result
wordForms.example: She managed the project effectively despite limited resources.
wordForms.rootDefinition: producing the desired or intended result

wordForms.definition: in the best possible way; perfectly
wordForms.example: Ideally, we would have more time to prepare.
wordForms.rootDefinition: perfect in every way; most suitable

wordForms.definition: to a greater extent than usual; especially
wordForms.example: The food was particularly good at that restaurant.

wordForms.definition: in a way that is important or noticeable
wordForms.example: The new law significantly changed working conditions.
wordForms.rootDefinition: important or large enough to be noticed

wordForms.definition: in a calm way, without complaining or rushing
wordForms.example: She waited patiently in the queue for over an hour.
wordForms.rootDefinition: able to wait calmly without getting annoyed

wordForms.definition: slowly, in small stages over time
wordForms.example: The situation gradually improved over the following months.
wordForms.rootDefinition: happening slowly over a period of time

wordForms.example: Students are expected to work independently on this project.
wordForms.rootDefinition: not relying on others; free from control

multipleChoiceExercises.sentence: She spoke ___ and everyone in the room understood her point.
The disease spread ___ over the next few weeks.
Each applicant was interviewed ___ by a panel of three judges.
He waited ___ while the doctor reviewed his test results.
Costs have risen ___ since the company expanded.
The children were not ___ supervised during the event.

transformExercises.sentence: ___, the company would relocate its headquarters to a bigger city. (IDEAL)
The economy has ___ improved since last year. (GRADUAL)
The new treatment has ___ reduced the number of cases. (SIGNIFICANT)
Students were assessed ___ rather than as a group. (INDIVIDUAL)
The work was ___ done to a very high standard. (COMPLETE)
